t.pickthall: Establish worship at the going down of the sun until the dark of night, and (the recital of) the Qur'an at dawn. Lo! (the recital of) the Qur'an at dawn is ever witnessed
t.arberry: Perform the prayer at the sinking of the sun to the darkening of the night and the recital of dawn; surely the recital of dawn is witnessed
t.hilali-khan: Perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat) from mid-day till the darkness of the night (i.e. the Zuhr, 'Asr, Maghrib, and 'Isha' prayers), and recite the Quran in the early dawn (i.e. the morning prayer). Verily, the recitation of the Quran in the early dawn is ever witnessed (attended by the angels in charge of mankind of the day and the night)
t.itani: Perform the prayer at the decline of the sun, until the darkness of the night; and the Quran at dawn. The Quran at dawn is witnessed.
t.mubarakpuri: Perform the Salah from midday till the darkness of the night, and recite the Qur'an in the early dawn. Verily, the recitation of the Qur'an in the early dawn is ever witnessed.
t.qarib: Establish prayer from the time the sun declines until the darkening of the night, and also [remember] the [Qur'an) recitation at dawn. The dawn recitation is certainly witnessed [by Us]
t.wahiduddin: Say your prayers from the decline of the sun, until nightfall; and at dawn -- the recitation at dawn is indeed witnessed
t.shakir: Keep up prayer from the declining of the sun till the darkness of the night and the morning recitation; surely the morning recitation is witnessed
t.haleem: So perform the regular prayers in the period from the time the sun is past its zenith till the darkness of the night, and [recite] the Quran at dawn- dawn recitation is always witnessed––
t.maariful: (O Prophet,) establish Salāh between the decline of the sun and the darkness of the night, and (establish) the recital at dawn. Surely, the recital at dawn is well attended
t.khattab: Observe the prayer from the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night and the dawn prayer, for certainly the dawn prayer is witnessed ˹by angels˺
t.ghali: Keep up the prayer at the sinking of the sun to the dusk of the night and (the reading) of the Qur'an at dawn; surely the reading of the Qur'an at dawn has been witnessed
t.irving: Keep up prayer from the decline of the sun, until twilight a night; and [observe] the Reading at daybreak, since reading [the Quran] at daybreak will be witnessed
t.iwq: Establish prayer at the decline of the sun from its meridian until the darkness of the night and also the Qur'an of dawn. Indeed, the recitation of dawn is ever witnessed

t_id.kemenag: Dirikanlah shalat dari sesudah matahari tergelincir sampai gelap malam dan (dirikanlah pula shalat) subuh. Sesungguhnya shalat subuh itu disaksikan (oleh malaikat).
t_id.sabiq: Kerjakanlah salat fardu, mulai matahari condong dari pertengahan langit sampai muncul kegelapan malam. Yaitu salat Zuhur, Asar, Magrib dan Isya. Kerjakan pula salat Fajar (Subuh) yang disaksikan oleh malaikat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dirikanlah salat dari sesudah matahari tergelincir) artinya sejak dari matahari tergelincir (sampai gelap malam) hingga kegelapan malam tiba; yang dimaksud adalah salat zuhur, asar, magrib dan isyak (dan bacaan di waktu fajar) yakni salat subuh (sesungguhnya bacaan di waktu fajar/salat subuh itu disaksikan) oleh malaikat-malaikat yang berjaga pada malam hari dan malaikat-malaikat yang berjaga pada siang hari.
t_id.iwq: Dirikanlah salat dari sesudah matahari tergelincir sampai gelap malam dan (dirikanlah pula salat) subuh. Sesungguhnya salat subuh itu disaksikan (oleh malaikat).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أقم الصلاة لدلوك الشمس» أي من وقت زوالها «إلى غسق الليل» إقبال ظلمته أي الظهر والعصر والمغرب والعشاء «وقرآن الفجر» صلاة الصبح «إن قرآن الفجر كان مشهودا» تشهده ملائكة الليل وملائكة النهار
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ لِدُلُوكِ ٱلشَّمۡسِ إِلَىٰ غَسَقِ ٱلَّيۡلِ وَقُرۡءَانَ ٱلۡفَجۡرِۖ إِنَّ قُرۡءَانَ ٱلۡفَجۡرِ كَانَ مَشۡهُودٗا (لِدُلُوكِ الشَّمْسِ: مِنْ وَقْتِ زَوَالِ الشَّمْسِ عِنْدَ الظَّهِيرَةِ, غَسَقِ اللَّيْلِ: ظُلْمَتِهِ, وَقُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ: صَلَاةَ الصُّبْحِ الَّتِي تُطَالُ فِيهَا قِرَاءَةُ القُرْآنِ, مَشْهُودًا: تَحْضُرُهَا مَلَائِكَةُ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dirikanlah salat dari sesudah matahari tergelincir) artinya sejak dari matahari tergelincir (sampai gelap malam) hingga kegelapan malam tiba; yang dimaksud adalah salat zuhur, asar, magrib dan isyak (dan bacaan di waktu fajar) yakni salat subuh (sesungguhnya bacaan di waktu fajar/salat subuh itu disaksikan) oleh malaikat-malaikat yang berjaga pada malam hari dan malaikat-malaikat yang berjaga pada siang hari

ar: وَمِنَ ٱلَّیۡلِ فَتَهَجَّدۡ بِهِۦ نَافِلَةࣰ لَّكَ عَسَىٰۤ أَن یَبۡعَثَكَ رَبُّكَ مَقَامࣰا مَّحۡمُودࣰا
ar_simple: ومن ٱلیل فتهجد به نافلة لك عسى أن یبعثك ربك مقاما محمودا

t.sahih-int: And from [part of] the night, pray with it as additional [worship] for you; it is expected that your Lord will resurrect you to a praised station
t.asad: And rise from thy sleep and pray during part of the night [as well], as a free offering from thee, and thy Sustainer may well raise thee to a glorious station [in the life to come]
t.yusuf-ali: And pray in the small watches of the morning: (it would be) an additional prayer (or spiritual profit) for thee: soon will thy Lord raise thee to a Station of Praise and Glory
t.pickthall: And some part of the night awake for it, a largess for thee. It may be that thy Lord will raise thee to a praised estate
t.arberry: And as for the night, keep vigil a part of it, as a work of supererogation for thee; it may be that thy Lord will raise thee up to a laudable station
t.hilali-khan: And in some parts of the night (also) offer the Salat (prayer) with it (i.e. recite the Quran in the prayer), as an additional prayer (Tahajjud optional prayer Nawafil) for you (O Muhammad SAW). It may be that your Lord will raise you to Maqaman Mahmuda (a station of praise and glory, i.e. the highest degree in Paradise)
t.itani: And keep vigil with it during parts of the night, as an extra prayer. Perhaps your Lord will raise you to a laudable position.
t.mubarakpuri: And in some parts of the night (also) offer the Salah with it as an additional prayer for you. It may be that your Lord will raise you to Maqam Mahmud.
t.qarib: Pray during the night, an extra voluntary prayer, as it may well be that your Lord will elevate you to a praiseworthy status
t.wahiduddin: And during the night wake up and pray, as an additional prayer: it may well be that your Lord will raise you to a station of praise and glory
t.shakir: And during a part of the night, pray Tahajjud beyond what is incumbent on you; maybe your Lord will raise you to a position of great glory
t.haleem: and dur-ing the night wake up and pray, as an extra offering of your own, so that your Lord may raise you to a [highly] praised status
t.maariful: And during the night, wake up for Salāh of tahajjud , an additional prayer for you. It is very likely that your Lord will place you at Praised Station
t.khattab: And rise at ˹the last˺ part of the night, offering additional prayers, so your Lord may raise you to a station of praise
t.ghali: And keep constant vigil with it (The Qur'an) (part) of the night (These are the late night supererogatory prayers) as an accordance for you; it may be that your Lord will make you rise again to a praised station
t.irving: At night, wake up and pray during it[s reading] as an extra bonus for yourself; perhaps your Lord will raise you once again to a praiseworthy standing
t.iwq: And from part of the night, pray with it as additional worship for you; it is expected that your Lord will resurrect you to a praised station

t_id.kemenag: Dan pada sebahagian malam hari bersembahyang tahajudlah kamu sebagai suatu ibadah tambahan bagimu; mudah-mudahan Tuhan-mu mengangkat kamu ke tempat yang terpuji.
t_id.sabiq: Bangunlah pada sebagian malam dan laksanakanlah salat tahajud sebagai ibadah tambahan atas salat lima waktu yang telah kamu laksanakan, dengan harapan agar Allah menempatkan kamu di hari kiamat pada tempat yang dipuji semua makhluk.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan pada sebagian malam hari bertahajudlah kamu) salatlah (dengan membacanya) yakni Alquran (sebagai suatu ibadah tambahan bagimu) sebagai amal fardu tambahan bagimu secara khusus bukan bagi umatmu, atau sebagai tambahan di samping salat-salat fardu (mudah-mudahan mengangkatmu) mendudukanmu (Rabbmu) di akhirat kelak (pada tempat yang terpuji) di mana semua orang yang terdahulu hingga orang yang kemudian memujimu karena kamu menduduki tempat tersebut; yaitu kedudukan memberi syafaat pada hari diputuskan-Nya segala perkara. Ayat berikut diturunkan sewaktu Allah memerintahkan Nabi saw. untuk melakukan hijrah.
t_id.iwq: Dan pada sebahagian malam hari bersembahyang tahajudlah kamu sebagai suatu ibadah tambahan bagimu; mudah-mudahan Tuhan-mu mengangkat kamu ke tempat yang terpuji.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ومن الليل فتهجد» فصلِّ «به» بالقرآن «نافلة لك» فريضة زائدة لك دون أمتك أو فضيلة على الصلوات المفروضة «عسى أن يبعثك» يقيمك «ربك» في الآخرة «مقاما محمودا» يحمدك فيه الأولون والآخرون وهو مقام الشفاعة في فصل القضاء، ونزل لما أمر بالهجرة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمِنَ ٱلَّيۡلِ فَتَهَجَّدۡ بِهِۦ نَافِلَةٗ لَّكَ عَسَىٰٓ أَن يَبۡعَثَكَ رَبُّكَ مَقَامٗا مَّحۡمُودٗا (فَتَهَجَّدْ: قُمْ مِنْ نَوْمِكَ فِي اللَّيْلِ لِلصَّلَاةِ, نَافِلَةً لَّكَ: زِيَادَةً لَكَ فِي عُلُوِّ القَدْرِ، وَرَفْعِ الدَّرَجَاتِ, مَقَامًا مَّحْمُودًا: مَقَامَ الشَّفَاعَةِ العُظْمَى لِفَصْلِ القَضَاءِ يَوْمَ القِيَامَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan pada sebagian malam hari bertahajudlah kamu) salatlah (dengan membacanya) yakni Alquran (sebagai suatu ibadah tambahan bagimu) sebagai amal fardu tambahan bagimu secara khusus bukan bagi umatmu, atau sebagai tambahan di samping salat-salat fardu (mudah-mudahan mengangkatmu) mendudukanmu (Rabbmu) di akhirat kelak (pada tempat yang terpuji) di mana semua orang yang terdahulu hingga orang yang kemudian memujimu karena kamu menduduki tempat tersebut; yaitu kedudukan memberi syafaat pada hari diputuskan-Nya segala perkara. Ayat berikut diturunkan sewaktu Allah memerintahkan Nabi saw. untuk melakukan hijrah

ar: وَقُل رَّبِّ أَدۡخِلۡنِی مُدۡخَلَ صِدۡقࣲ وَأَخۡرِجۡنِی مُخۡرَجَ صِدۡقࣲ وَٱجۡعَل لِّی مِن لَّدُنكَ سُلۡطَـٰنࣰا نَّصِیرࣰا
ar_simple: وقل رب أدخلنی مدخل صدق وأخرجنی مخرج صدق وٱجعل لی من لدنك سلطنا نصیرا

t.sahih-int: And say, "My Lord, cause me to enter a sound entrance and to exit a sound exit and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority
t.asad: And say [in thy prayer]: "O my Sustainer! Cause me to enter [upon whatever I may do] in a manner. true and sincere, and cause me to leave [it] in a manner true and sincere, and grant me, out of Thy grace, sustaining strength
t.yusuf-ali: Say: "O my Lord! Let my entry be by the Gate of Truth and Honour, and likewise my exit by the Gate of Truth and Honour; and grant me from Thy Presence an authority to aid (me)
t.pickthall: And say: My Lord! Cause me to come in with a firm incoming and to go out with a firm outgoing. And give me from Thy presence a sustaining Power
t.arberry: And say: 'My Lord, lead me in with a just ingoing, and lead me out with a just outgoing; grant me authority from Thee, to help me
t.hilali-khan: And say (O Muhammad SAW): My Lord! Let my entry (to the city of Al-Madinah) be good, and likewise my exit (from the city of Makkah) be good. And grant me from You an authority to help me (or a firm sign or a proof)
t.itani: And say, “My Lord, lead me in through an entry of truth, and lead me out through an exit of truth, and grant me from You a supporting power.”
t.mubarakpuri: And say: "My Lord! Let my entry be good, and (likewise) my exit be good. And grant me from You a helping authority,"
t.qarib: Say, "My Lord, Cause me to enter [in all I do] with truth, and cause me to finish with truth, and grant me strength out of Your sustaining grace
t.wahiduddin: Say, "Lord, grant me an honourable entrance and an honourable exit, and sustain me with Your power
t.shakir: And say: My Lord! make me to enter a goodly entering, and cause me to go forth a goodly going forth, and grant me from near Thee power to assist (me)
t.haleem: Say, ‘My Lord, make me go in truthfully, and come out truthfully, and grant me supporting authority from You.’
t.maariful: And say, “O my Lord, make me enter a rightful entrance and make me exit a rightful exit, and grant me from Your Own a power, favored (by You).”
t.khattab: And say, “My Lord! Grant me an honourable entrance and an honourable exit and give me a supporting authority from Yourself.”
t.ghali: And say, "Lord! Cause me to enter a sincere entry and cause me to go out a sincere outgoing, and make for me, from very close to You, a ready vindicating all - binding authority
t.irving: SAY: "My Lord, let me enter through a proper entrance and leave by an honest exit! Grant me supporting authority from Your presence
t.iwq: And say, "My Lord, cause me to enter a sound entrance and to exit a sound exit and grant me from Yourself a supporting authority

t_id.kemenag: Dan katakanlah: "Ya Tuhan-ku, masukkanlah aku secara masuk yang benar dan keluarkanlah (pula) aku secara keluar yang benar dan berikanlah kepadaku dari sisi Engkau kekuasaan yang menolong.
t_id.sabiq: Katakanlah, "Ya Tuhan, masukkanlah aku dalam segala situasi dan kondisi dengan cara yang Engkau ridai dan dengan cara yang terhormat. Keluarkanlah aku dari mana saja dengan keridaan dan kemuliaan-Mu pula. Berikanlah kekuatan kepadaku, dari karunia-Mu, yang dapat membelaku melawan musuh."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan katakanlah, "Ya Rabbku! Masukkanlah aku) ke Madinah (dengan cara yang baik) yakni dengan cara memasukkan yang disukai di mana aku tidak melihat sewaktu masuk hal-hal yang tidak aku sukai (dan keluarkanlah aku) dari Mekah (dengan cara yang baik) dengan cara mengeluarkan yang membuat hatiku tidak berpaling lagi kepadanya (dan berikanlah kepadaku dari sisi Engkau kekuasaan yang menolong.") kekuatan yang dapat membantuku untuk dapat mengalahkan musuh-musuh-Mu.
t_id.iwq: Dan katakanlah: "Ya Tuhan-ku, masukkanlah aku secara masuk yang benar dan keluarkanlah (pula) aku secara keluar yang benar dan berikanlah kepadaku dari sisi Engkau kekuasaan yang menolong.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقل ربِّ أدخلني» المدينة «مُدخل صدق» إدخالاً مرضيا لا أرى فيه ما أكره «وأخرجني» من مكة «مُخرج صدق» إخراجا لا ألتفت بقلبي إليها «واجعل لي من لدنك سلطانا نصيرا» قوة تنصرني بها على أعدائك
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقُل رَّبِّ أَدۡخِلۡنِي مُدۡخَلَ صِدۡقٖ وَأَخۡرِجۡنِي مُخۡرَجَ صِدۡقٖ وَٱجۡعَل لِّي مِن لَّدُنكَ سُلۡطَٰنٗا نَّصِيرٗا (مُدْخَلَ صِدْقٍ: أَيْ: إِدْخَالًا مَرْضِيًّا, سُلْطَانًا: حُجَّةً، وَقُوَّةً)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan katakanlah, "Ya Rabbku! Masukkanlah aku) ke Madinah (dengan cara yang baik) yakni dengan cara memasukkan yang disukai di mana aku tidak melihat sewaktu masuk hal-hal yang tidak aku sukai (dan keluarkanlah aku) dari Mekah (dengan cara yang baik) dengan cara mengeluarkan yang membuat hatiku tidak berpaling lagi kepadanya (dan berikanlah kepadaku dari sisi Engkau kekuasaan yang menolong.") kekuatan yang dapat membantuku untuk dapat mengalahkan musuh-musuh-Mu

ar: وَقُلۡ جَاۤءَ ٱلۡحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ ٱلۡبَـٰطِلُۚ إِنَّ ٱلۡبَـٰطِلَ كَانَ زَهُوقࣰا
ar_simple: وقل جاء ٱلحق وزهق ٱلبطل إن ٱلبطل كان زهوقا

t.sahih-int: And say, "Truth has come, and falsehood has departed. Indeed is falsehood, [by nature], ever bound to depart
t.asad: And say: "The truth has now come [to light], and falsehood has withered away: for, behold, all falsehood is bound to wither away
t.yusuf-ali: And say: "Truth has (now) arrived, and Falsehood perished: for Falsehood is (by its nature) bound to perish
t.pickthall: And say: Truth hath come and falsehood hath vanished away. Lo! falsehood is ever bound to vanish
t.arberry: And say: 'The truth has come, and falsehood has vanished away; surely falsehood is ever certain to vanish
t.hilali-khan: And say: "Truth (i.e. Islamic Monotheism or this Quran or Jihad against polytheists) has come and Batil (falsehood, i.e. Satan or polytheism, etc.) has vanished. Surely! Batil is ever bound to vanish
t.itani: And say, “The truth has come, and falsehood has withered away; for falsehood is bound to wither away.”
t.mubarakpuri: And say: "Truth has come and falsehood has vanished. Surely, falsehood is ever bound to vanish."
t.qarib: And say, "The Truth has come, and falsehood has vanished, for, falsehood is bound to vanish
t.wahiduddin: Say, "Truth has come and falsehood has disappeared. Falsehood is always bound to wither away
t.shakir: And say: The truth has come and the falsehood has vanished; surely falsehood is a vanishing (thing)
t.haleem: And say, ‘The truth has come, and falsehood has passed away: falsehood is bound to pass away.’
t.maariful: And say, “Truth has come and falsehood has vanished. Falsehood is surely bound to vanish.”
t.khattab: And declare, “The truth has come and falsehood has vanished. Indeed, falsehood is bound to vanish.”
t.ghali: And say, "The Truth has come and untruth has expired; surely the untruth has been certain to expire." (Literally: ever-expiring)
t.irving: And SAY (too): "Truth has come and falsehood vanished; falsehood is so perishable
t.iwq: And say, "Truth has come, and falsehood has departed. Indeed is falsehood, by nature, ever bound to depart

t_id.kemenag: Dan katakanlah: "Yang benar telah datang dan yang batil telah lenyap". Sesungguhnya yang batil itu adalah sesuatu yang pasti lenyap.
t_id.sabiq: Berikanlah peringatan kepada kaummu yang musyrik dengan mengatakan, "Kebenaran (yaitu ajaran tauhid, agama yang benar dan keadilan) telah datang, dan kebatilan (kesyirikan, agama yang tidak benar dan kezaliman) telah lenyap. Sesungguhnya kebatilan itu akan selalu lenyap."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan katakanlah) sewaktu kamu memasuki kembali Mekah ("Yang benar telah datang) yakni agama Islam (dan yang batil telah lenyap.") batilnya kekafiran telah lenyap. (Sesungguhnya yang batil itu adalah sesuatu yang pasti lenyap) akan surut, lalu lenyap. Memang ketika Nabi saw. memasuki kota Mekah, beliau menemukan tiga ratus enam puluh berhala berada di sekitar Kakbah, kemudian Nabi saw. menusukinya dengan tongkat yang berada di tangannya sehingga semuanya runtuh, seraya mengucapkan kalimat tadi. Demikianlah menurut hadis yang diriwayatkan oleh Bukhari dan Muslim.
t_id.iwq: Dan katakanlah: "Yang benar telah datang dan yang batil telah lenyap". Sesungguhnya yang batil itu adalah sesuatu yang pasti lenyap.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (وقل) عند دخولك مكة (جاء الحق) الإسلام (وزهق الباطل) بطل الكفر (إن الباطل كان زهوقا) مضمحلاً زائلاً "" وقد دخلها صلى الله عليه وسلم وحول البيت ثلاثمائة وستون صنما فجعل يطعنها بعود في يده ويقول ذلك حتى سقطت "" رواه الشيخان
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقُلۡ جَآءَ ٱلۡحَقُّ وَزَهَقَ ٱلۡبَٰطِلُۚ إِنَّ ٱلۡبَٰطِلَ كَانَ زَهُوقٗا (وَزَهَقَ: بَطَلَ، وَاضْمَحَلَّ, زَهُوقًا: لَا بَقَاءَ لَهُ، وَلَا ثَبَاتَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan katakanlah) sewaktu kamu memasuki kembali Mekah ("Yang benar telah datang) yakni agama Islam (dan yang batil telah lenyap.") batilnya kekafiran telah lenyap. (Sesungguhnya yang batil itu adalah sesuatu yang pasti lenyap) akan surut, lalu lenyap. Memang ketika Nabi saw. memasuki kota Mekah, beliau menemukan tiga ratus enam puluh berhala berada di sekitar Kakbah, kemudian Nabi saw. menusukinya dengan tongkat yang berada di tangannya sehingga semuanya runtuh, seraya mengucapkan kalimat tadi. Demikianlah menurut hadis yang diriwayatkan oleh Bukhari dan Muslim

ar: وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ مَا هُوَ شِفَاۤءࣱ وَرَحۡمَةࣱ لِّلۡمُؤۡمِنِینَ وَلَا یَزِیدُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِینَ إِلَّا خَسَارࣰا
ar_simple: وننزل من ٱلقرءان ما هو شفاء ورحمة للمؤمنین ولا یزید ٱلظلمین إلا خسارا

t.sahih-int: And We send down of the Qur'an that which is healing and mercy for the believers, but it does not increase the wrongdoers except in loss
t.asad: THUS, step by step, We bestow from on high through this Qur'an all that gives health [to the spirit] and is a grace unto those who believe [in Us], the while it only adds to the ruin of evildoers
t.yusuf-ali: We send down (stage by stage) in the Qur'an that which is a healing and a mercy to those who believe: to the unjust it causes nothing but loss after loss
t.pickthall: And We reveal of the Qur'an that which is a healing and a mercy for believers though it increase the evil-doers in naught save ruin
t.arberry: And We send down, of the Koran, that which is a healing and a mercy to the believers; and the unbelievers it increases not, except in loss
t.hilali-khan: And We send down from the Quran that which is a healing and a mercy to those who believe (in Islamic Monotheism and act on it), and it increases the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers) nothing but loss
t.itani: We send down in the Quran healing and mercy for the believers, but it increases the wrongdoers only in loss.
t.mubarakpuri: And We send down of the Qur'an that which is a cure and a mercy to the believers, and it increases the wrongdoers in nothing but loss.
t.qarib: We send down from the Qur'an what is a healing and mercy to the believers. And it only increases the unjust in their loss
t.wahiduddin: We send down in the Quran that which is healing and a mercy to those who believe; as for the evil-doers, it only increases their loss
t.shakir: And We reveal of the Quran that which is a healing and a mercy to the believers, and it adds only to the perdition of the unjust
t.haleem: We send down the Quran as healing and mercy to those who believe; as for those who disbelieve, it only increases their loss
t.maariful: We reveal the Qur’ān, which is cure and mercy for the believers; and it adds nothing to the unjust but loss
t.khattab: We send down the Quran as a healing and mercy for the believers, but it only increases the wrongdoers in loss
t.ghali: And We are sending down, of the Qur'an, that which is a cure and a mercy to the believers; and it increases the unjust in nothing except in greater loss
t.irving: We send down something from the Quran [to serve] as healing and a mercy for believers, while wrongdoers are only increased in loss
t.iwq: And We send down of the Qur'an that which is healing and mercy for those who believe, but it does not increase those who do wrong except in loss

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami turunkan dari Al Quran suatu yang menjadi penawar dan rahmat bagi orang-orang yang beriman dan Al Quran itu tidaklah menambah kepada orang-orang yang zalim selain kerugian.
t_id.sabiq: Bagaimana kebenaran itu tidak akan menjadi kuat, sedang Kami telah menurunkan al-Qur'ân sebagai penawar keraguan yang ada dalam dada, dan rahmat bagi siapa yang beriman kepadanya. Al-Qur'ân itu tidak menambah apa-apa kepada orang-orang yang zalim selain kerugian, oleh sebab kekufuran mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami turunkan dari) huruf min di sini menunjukkan makna bayan atau penjelasan (Alquran suatu yang menjadi penawar) dari kesesatan (dan rahmat bagi orang-orang yang beriman) kepadanya (dan Alquran itu tidaklah menambah kepada orang-orang yang zalim) yakni orang-orang yang kafir (selain kerugian) dikarenakan kekafiran mereka
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami turunkan dari Al Al-Quran suatu yang menjadi penawar dan rahmat bagi orang-orang yang beriman dan Al Al-Quran itu tidaklah menambah kepada orang-orang yang zalim selain kerugian.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وننزل من» للبيان «القرآن ما هو شفاء» من الضلالة «ورحمة للمؤمنين» به «ولا يزيد الظالمين» الكافرين «إلا خسارا» لكفرهم به
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَنُنَزِّلُ مِنَ ٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ مَا هُوَ شِفَآءٞ وَرَحۡمَةٞ لِّلۡمُؤۡمِنِينَ وَلَا يَزِيدُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ إِلَّا خَسَارٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami turunkan dari) huruf min di sini menunjukkan makna bayan atau penjelasan (Alquran suatu yang menjadi penawar) dari kesesatan (dan rahmat bagi orang-orang yang beriman) kepadanya (dan Alquran itu tidaklah menambah kepada orang-orang yang zalim) yakni orang-orang yang kafir (selain kerugian) dikarenakan kekafiran mereka

ar: وَإِذَاۤ أَنۡعَمۡنَا عَلَى ٱلۡإِنسَـٰنِ أَعۡرَضَ وَنَءَا بِجَانِبِهِۦ وَإِذَا مَسَّهُ ٱلشَّرُّ كَانَ یَءُوسࣰا
ar_simple: وإذا أنعمنا على ٱلإنسن أعرض ونءا بجانبه وإذا مسه ٱلشر كان یءوسا

t.sahih-int: And when We bestow favor upon the disbeliever, he turns away and distances himself; and when evil touches him, he is ever despairing
t.asad: for [it often happens that] when We bestow Our blessings upon man, he turns away and arrogantly keeps aloof [from any thought of Us]; and when evil fortune touches him, he abandons all hope
t.yusuf-ali: Yet when We bestow Our favours on man, he turns away and becomes remote on his side (instead of coming to Us), and when evil seizes him he gives himself up to despair
t.pickthall: And when We make life pleasant unto man, he turneth away and is averse; and when ill toucheth him he is in despair
t.arberry: And when We bless man, he turns away, and withdraws aside; but when evil visits him, he is in despair
t.hilali-khan: And when We bestow Our Grace on man (the disbeliever), he turns away and becomes arrogant, far away from the Right Path. And when evil touches him he is in great despair
t.itani: When We bless the human being, he turns away and distances himself. But when adversity touches him, he is in despair.
t.mubarakpuri: And when We bestow Our grace on man, he turns away and becomes arrogant. And when evil touches him, he is in great despair.
t.qarib: for, when We bless a human being, he turns away, and arrogantly distances himself; and when he encounters calamity he despairs
t.wahiduddin: When We bestow a favour upon a person, he turns his back and draws aside; and when evil afflicts him he gives himself up to despair
t.shakir: And when We bestow favor on man, he turns aside and behaves proudly, and when evil afflicts him, he is despairing
t.haleem: When We favour man he turns arrogantly to one side, but when harm touches him, he falls into despair
t.maariful: When We bestow Our favor upon man, he avoids (to recognize it) and keeps himself far aside (from obedience), and when some evil touches him, he is in total despair
t.khattab: When We grant people Our favours, they turn away, acting arrogantly. But when touched with evil, they lose all hope
t.ghali: And when We (confer) favors upon man, he veers away and retires aside; and when evil touches him, he has been constantly despairing
t.irving: Whenever We show man some favor, he avoids it and drifts off to one side; while whenever evil touches him, he acts desperate
t.iwq: And when We bestow favor upon the disbeliever, he turns away and distances himself; and when evil touches him, he is ever despairing

t_id.kemenag: Dan apabila Kami berikan kesenangan kepada manusia niscaya berpalinglah dia; dan membelakang dengan sikap yang sombong; dan apabila dia ditimpa kesusahan niscaya dia berputus asa.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya perasaan bangga dan putus asa merupakan tabiat manusia. Apabila ia Kami berikan nikmat kesehatan dan kelapangan, mereka malah tidak mau berzikir dan berdoa kepada Kami, serta menjauh dari Kami dengan sombong dan berbangga diri. Dan jika ia ditimpa kesusahan seperti sakit dan kemiskinan, mereka putus asa dari rahmat Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan apabila Kami berikan kesenangan kepada manusia) yang kafir (niscaya berpalinglah dia) daripada bersyukur (dan membelakangkan badannya) yakni membelakangkan tubuhnya dengan sikap yang sombong (dan apabila dia ditimpa kesusahan) kemiskinan dan kesengsaraan (niscaya dia berputus asa) dari rahmat Allah.
t_id.iwq: Dan apabila Kami berikan kesenangan kepada manusia niscaya berpalinglah dia; dan membelakang dengan sikap yang sombong; dan apabila dia ditimpa kesusahan niscaya dia berputus asa.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وإذا أنعمنا على الإنسان» الكافر «أعرض» عن الشكر «ونأى بجانبه» ثنى عطفه متبخترا «وإذا مسه الشر» الفقر والشدة «كان يؤوسا» قنوطا من رحمة الله
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِذَآ أَنۡعَمۡنَا عَلَى ٱلۡإِنسَٰنِ أَعۡرَضَ وَنَـَٔا بِجَانِبِهِۦ وَإِذَا مَسَّهُ ٱلشَّرُّ كَانَ يَـُٔوسٗا (وَنَأَى بِجَانِبِهِ: تَبَاعَدَ عَنْ طَاعَةِ رَبِّهِ كِبْرًا، وَعِنَادًا, يَؤُوسًا: قَنُوطًا مِنْ رَحْمَةِ اللهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan apabila Kami berikan kesenangan kepada manusia) yang kafir (niscaya berpalinglah dia) daripada bersyukur (dan membelakangkan badannya) yakni membelakangkan tubuhnya dengan sikap yang sombong (dan apabila dia ditimpa kesusahan) kemiskinan dan kesengsaraan (niscaya dia berputus asa) dari rahmat Allah

ar: قُلۡ كُلࣱّ یَعۡمَلُ عَلَىٰ شَاكِلَتِهِۦ فَرَبُّكُمۡ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَنۡ هُوَ أَهۡدَىٰ سَبِیلࣰا
ar_simple: قل كل یعمل على شاكلته فربكم أعلم بمن هو أهدى سبیلا

t.sahih-int: Say, "Each works according to his manner, but your Lord is most knowing of who is best guided in way
t.asad: Say: "Everyone acts in a manner peculiar to himself -and your Sustainer is fully aware as to who has chosen the best path
t.yusuf-ali: Say: "Everyone acts according to his own disposition: But your Lord knows best who it is that is best guided on the Way
t.pickthall: Say: Each one doth according to his rule of conduct, and thy Lord is Best Aware of him whose way is right
t.arberry: Say: 'Every man works according to his own manner; but your Lord knows very well what man is best guided as to the way
t.hilali-khan: Say (O Muhammad SAW to mankind): "Each one does according to Shakilatihi (i.e. his way or his religion or his intentions, etc.), and your Lord knows best of him whose path (religion, etc.) is right
t.itani: Say, “Each does according to his disposition. Your Lord knows best who is better guided in the way.”
t.mubarakpuri: Say: "Each one does according to Shakilatihi, and your Lord knows best of him whose path is right."
t.qarib: Say, "Each one acts in a manner reflecting his own character. Your Lord is fully aware as to who is guided to the best path
t.wahiduddin: Say to them, "Everyone acts in his own way, and your Lord knows best who is rightly guided
t.shakir: Say: Every one acts according to his manner; but your Lord best knows who is best guided in the path
t.haleem: Say, ‘Everyone does things their own way, but your Lord is fully aware of who follows the best-guided path.’
t.maariful: Say, “Everyone acts in his own style. So, your Lord knows best which one is better guided in his way.”
t.khattab: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Everyone acts in their own way. But your Lord knows best whose way is rightly guided.”
t.ghali: Say, "Each one does according to his own manner; yet your Lord is He who knows best whoever is best guided to the way
t.irving: SAY: Everyone acts according to his own disposition. Your Lord is quite Aware as to who is best guided along the Way
t.iwq: Say, "Each works according to his manner, but your Lord is most knowing of who is best guided in way

t_id.kemenag: Katakanlah: "Tiap-tiap orang berbuat menurut keadaannya masing-masing". Maka Tuhanmu lebih mengetahui siapa yang lebih benar jalannya.
t_id.sabiq: Katakanlah, wahai Muhammad, kepada orang-orang kafir Quraisy sebagai ungkapan rasa tidak senangmu terhadap kejahatan dan pertikaian, "Masing-masing kita berbuat dan berjalan sesuai dengan jalannya." Tuhanmu Maha Mengetahui--dengan ilmu pengetahuan yang tidak tertandingi--tentang orang-orang yang lebih benar jalannya dan selalu berbuat kebenaran. Mereka akan mendapatkan pahala yang besar. Allah juga Maha Mengetahui siapa yang lebih sesat jalannya dan akan diberi balasan sesuai dengan apa yang ia perbuat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah, "Tiap-tiap orang) di antara kami dan kalian (berbuat menurut keadaannya masing-masing) yakni menurut caranya sendiri-sendiri (Maka Rabb kalian lebih mengetahui siapa yang lebih benar jalannya") maka Dia akan memberi pahala kepada orang yang lebih benar jalannya.
t_id.iwq: Katakanlah: "Tiap-tiap orang berbuat menurut keadaannya masing-masing". Maka Tuhanmu lebih mengetahui siapa yang lebih benar jalannya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قل كل» منا ومنكم «يعمل على شاكلته» طريقته «فربكم أعلم بمن هو أهدى سبيلاً» طريقا فيثيبه
tafsir.siraj-ar: قُلۡ كُلّٞ يَعۡمَلُ عَلَىٰ شَاكِلَتِهِۦ فَرَبُّكُمۡ أَعۡلَمُ بِمَنۡ هُوَ أَهۡدَىٰ سَبِيلٗا (شَاكِلَتِهِ: طَرِيقَتِهِ، وَمَا يَلِيقُ بِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah, "Tiap-tiap orang) di antara kami dan kalian (berbuat menurut keadaannya masing-masing) yakni menurut caranya sendiri-sendiri (Maka Rabb kalian lebih mengetahui siapa yang lebih benar jalannya") maka Dia akan memberi pahala kepada orang yang lebih benar jalannya

ar: وَیَسۡءَلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلرُّوحِۖ قُلِ ٱلرُّوحُ مِنۡ أَمۡرِ رَبِّی وَمَاۤ أُوتِیتُم مِّنَ ٱلۡعِلۡمِ إِلَّا قَلِیلࣰا
ar_simple: ویسءلونك عن ٱلروح قل ٱلروح من أمر ربی وما أوتیتم من ٱلعلم إلا قلیلا

t.sahih-int: And they ask you, [O Muhammad], about the soul. Say, "The soul is of the affair of my Lord. And mankind have not been given of knowledge except a little
t.asad: AND THEY will ask thee about [the nature of] divine inspiration. Say: "This inspiration [comes] at my Sustainer's behest; and [you cannot understand its nature, O men, since] you have been granted very little of [real] knowledge
t.yusuf-ali: They ask thee concerning the Spirit (of inspiration). Say: "The Spirit (cometh) by command of my Lord: of knowledge it is only a little that is communicated to you, (O men)
t.pickthall: They are asking thee concerning the Spirit. Say: The Spirit is by command of my Lord, and of knowledge ye have been vouchsafed but little
t.arberry: They will question thee concerning the Spirit. Say: 'The Spirit is of the bidding of my Lord. You have been given of knowledge nothing except a little
t.hilali-khan: And they ask you (O Muhammad SAW) concerning the Ruh (the Spirit); Say: "The Ruh (the Spirit): it is one of the things, the knowledge of which is only with my Lord. And of knowledge, you (mankind) have been given only a little
t.itani: And they ask you about the Spirit. Say, “The Spirit belongs to the domain of my Lord; and you were given only little knowledge.”
t.mubarakpuri: And they ask you concerning the Ruh (the spirit). Say: "The Ruh (the spirit) is one of the things, the knowledge of which is only with my Lord. And of knowledge, you (mankind) have been given only a little."
t.qarib: And they will ask you about the spirit. Say, "The spirit is within my Lord's domain, and you have been given but little knowledge
t.wahiduddin: They question you about the Spirit. Say, "The Spirit is at my Lord's command, and you have been granted but little knowledge
t.shakir: And they ask you about the soul. Say: The soul is one of the commands of my Lord, and you are not given aught of knowledge but a little
t.haleem: [Prophet], they ask you about the Spirit. Say, ‘The Spirit is part of my Lord’s domain. You have only been given a little knowledge.’
t.maariful: And they ask you about the soul. Say, “The soul is something from the command of my Lord, and you are not given from the knowledge but a little.”
t.khattab: They ask you ˹O Prophet˺ about the spirit. Say, “Its nature is known only to my Lord, and you ˹O humanity˺ have been given but little knowledge.”
t.ghali: And they ask you about the Spirit. Say, "The Spirit is of the Command of my Lord; and in no way have you been brought of knowledge except a little
t.irving: They will ask you about the Spirit. SAY: "The Spirit [operates] at my Lord´s command, while you have been given only a smattering of knowledge
t.iwq: And they ask you, O Muhammad, about the soul. Say, "The soul is of the affair of my Lord. And mankind have not been given of knowledge except a little

t_id.kemenag: Dan mereka bertanya kepadamu tentang roh. Katakanlah: "Roh itu termasuk urusan Tuhan-ku, dan tidaklah kamu diberi pengetahuan melainkan sedikit".
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Muhammad, kaummu yang mendapat nasihat dari orang-orang Yahudi bertanya kepadamu tentang hakikat rûh (roh). Katakan, "Hanya Allah yang mengetahui ihwal roh. Aku hanya diberi sedikit sekali dari ilmu Allah tentang hal itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka bertanya kepadamu) yaitu orang-orang Yahudi (tentang roh,) yang karenanya jasad ini dapat hidup ("Katakanlah) kepada mereka! ('Roh itu termasuk urusan Rabbku) artinya termasuk ilmu-Nya oleh karenanya kalian tidak akan dapat mengetahuinya (dan tidaklah kalian diberi pengetahuan melainkan sedikit.'") dibandingkan dengan ilmu Allah swt.
t_id.iwq: Dan mereka bertanya kepadamu tentang roh. Katakanlah: "Roh itu termasuk urusan Tuhan-ku, dan tidaklah kamu diberi pengetahuan melainkan sedikit".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ويسألونك» أي اليهود «عن الروح» الذي يحيا به البدن «قل» لهم «الروح من أمر ربي» أي علمه لا تعلمونه «وما أويتم من العلم إلا قليلاً» بالنسبة إلى علمه تعالى
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَيَسۡـَٔلُونَكَ عَنِ ٱلرُّوحِۖ قُلِ ٱلرُّوحُ مِنۡ أَمۡرِ رَبِّي وَمَآ أُوتِيتُم مِّنَ ٱلۡعِلۡمِ إِلَّا قَلِيلٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka bertanya kepadamu) yaitu orang-orang Yahudi (tentang roh,) yang karenanya jasad ini dapat hidup ("Katakanlah) kepada mereka! ('Roh itu termasuk urusan Rabbku) artinya termasuk ilmu-Nya oleh karenanya kalian tidak akan dapat mengetahuinya (dan tidaklah kalian diberi pengetahuan melainkan sedikit.'") dibandingkan dengan ilmu Allah swt

ar: وَلَئِن شِئۡنَا لَنَذۡهَبَنَّ بِٱلَّذِیۤ أَوۡحَیۡنَاۤ إِلَیۡكَ ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُ لَكَ بِهِۦ عَلَیۡنَا وَكِیلًا
ar_simple: ولئن شئنا لنذهبن بٱلذی أوحینا إلیك ثم لا تجد لك به علینا وكیلا

t.sahih-int: And if We willed, We could surely do away with that which We revealed to you. Then you would not find for yourself concerning it an advocate against Us
t.asad: And if We so willed, We could indeed take away whatever We have revealed unto thee, and in that [state of need] thou wouldst find none to plead in thy behalf before Us
t.yusuf-ali: If it were Our Will, We could take away that which We have sent thee by inspiration: then wouldst thou find none to plead thy affair in that matter as against Us
t.pickthall: And if We willed We could withdraw that which We have revealed unto thee, then wouldst thou find no guardian for thee against Us in respect thereof
t.arberry: If We willed, We could take away that We have revealed to thee, then thou wouldst find none thereover to guard thee against Us
t.hilali-khan: And if We willed We could surely take away that which We have revealed to you by inspiration (i.e. this Quran). Then you would find no protector for you against Us in that respect
t.itani: If We willed, We could take away what We revealed to you. Then you will find for yourself no protecting guardian against Us.
t.mubarakpuri: And if We willed, We could surely take away that which We have revealed to you. Then you would find no protector for you against Us in that respect.
t.qarib: And if We so desired, We could easily take away whatever We have revealed to you, and in that [case] you would not find anyone to plead your case with us
t.wahiduddin: If We pleased, We would certainly take away that which We have revealed to you, then you would find no guardian for you against Us
t.shakir: And if We please, We should certainly take away that which We have revealed to you, then you would not find for it any protector against Us
t.haleem: If We pleased, We could take away what We have revealed to you- then you would find no one to plead for you against Us
t.maariful: And if We so will, We will surely take away what We have revealed to you, then you shall find no one to rely upon against Us in this matter
t.khattab: If We willed, We could have certainly taken away what We have revealed to you ˹O Prophet˺—then you would find none to guarantee its return from Us—
t.ghali: And indeed in case We decided, indeed We would definitely go away with that which We have revealed to you; thereafter you would not find for you a constant trustee there over against Us
t.irving: If We so wished, We would take away anything We have inspired you with; then you would not find anyone you could rely on against Us with it
t.iwq: And if We willed, We could surely do away with that which We revealed to you. Then you would not find for yourself concerning it an advocate against Us

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya jika Kami menghendaki, niscaya Kami lenyapkan apa yang telah Kami wahyukan kepadamu, dan dengan pelenyapan itu, kamu tidak akan mendapatkan seorang pembelapun terhadap Kami,
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya jika Kami berkehendak menghapus al-Qur'ân yang telah Kami wahyukan kepadamu dari dalam hatimu, niscaya akan Kami lakukan. Setelah itu kamu tidak akan menemukan seorang pun yang dapat membela dan menolongmu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya jika) lam di sini bermakna qasam (Kami menghendaki, niscaya Kami lenyapkan apa yang telah Kami wahyukan kepadamu) yakni Alquran; seumpamanya Dia menghapuskannya dari ingatanmu dan dari mushaf-mushaf yang ada (dan dengan pelenyapan itu kamu tidak akan mendapatkan seorang pembela pun terhadap Kami).
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya jika Kami menghendaki, niscaya Kami lenyapkan apa yang telah Kami wahyukan kepadamu, dan dengan pelenyapan itu, kamu tidak akan mendapatkan seorang pembelapun terhadap Kami,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولئن» لام قسم «شئنا لنذهبن بالذي أوحينا إليك» أي القرآن بأن نمحوه من الصدور والمصاحف «ثم لا تجد لك به علينا وكيلاً»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَئِن شِئۡنَا لَنَذۡهَبَنَّ بِٱلَّذِيٓ أَوۡحَيۡنَآ إِلَيۡكَ ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُ لَكَ بِهِۦ عَلَيۡنَا وَكِيلًا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya jika) lam di sini bermakna qasam (Kami menghendaki, niscaya Kami lenyapkan apa yang telah Kami wahyukan kepadamu) yakni Alquran; seumpamanya Dia menghapuskannya dari ingatanmu dan dari mushaf-mushaf yang ada (dan dengan pelenyapan itu kamu tidak akan mendapatkan seorang pembela pun terhadap Kami)

ar: إِلَّا رَحۡمَةࣰ مِّن رَّبِّكَۚ إِنَّ فَضۡلَهُۥ كَانَ عَلَیۡكَ كَبِیرࣰا
ar_simple: إلا رحمة من ربك إن فضله كان علیك كبیرا

t.sahih-int: Except [We have left it with you] as a mercy from your Lord. Indeed, His favor upon you has ever been great
t.asad: [Thou art spared] only by thy Sustainer's grace: behold, His favour towards thee is great indeed
t.yusuf-ali: Except for Mercy from thy Lord: for his bounty is to thee (indeed) great
t.pickthall: (It is naught) save mercy from thy Lord. Lo! His kindness unto thee was ever great
t.arberry: excepting by some mercy of thy Lord; surly His favour to thee is great
t.hilali-khan: Except as a Mercy from your Lord. Verily! His Grace unto you (O Muhammad SAW) is ever great
t.itani: Except through a mercy from your Lord. His favors upon you have been great.
t.mubarakpuri: Except as a mercy from your Lord. Verily, His grace unto you is ever great.
t.qarib: other than mercy from your Lord, His favor towards you is huge
t.wahiduddin: except through the special mercy of your Lord. His favours towards you has been great indeed
t.shakir: But on account of mercy from your Lord-- surely His grace to you is abundant
t.haleem: if it were not for your Lord’s mercy: His favour to you has been truly great
t.maariful: But it is a mercy from your Lord. Great is indeed His favor upon you
t.khattab: had it not been for the mercy of your Lord. Indeed, His favour upon you is immense
t.ghali: Excepting (by some) mercy from your Lord; surely His Grace over you has been great
t.irving: except for some mercy from your Lord, His bounty towards you has been great
t.iwq: Except We have left it with you as a mercy from your Lord. Indeed, His favor upon you has ever been great

t_id.kemenag: kecuali karena rahmat dari Tuhanmu. Sesungguhnya karunia-Nya atasmu adalah besar.
t_id.sabiq: Akan tetapi al-Qur'ân itu Kami jadikan abadi sebagai rahmat dari Tuhanmu, karena karunia Allah kepadamu dalam mukjizat ini sangatlah besar.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kecuali) tetapi sengaja Kami menetapkannya (karena rahmat dari Rabbmu. Sesungguhnya karunia-Nya atasmu adalah besar) agung karena Dia telah menurunkan Alquran kepadamu dan Dia memberimu kedudukan yang terpuji serta keutamaan-keutamaan lain yang Dia berikan kepadamu.
t_id.iwq: kecuali karena rahmat dari Tuhanmu. Sesungguhnya karunia-Nya atasmu adalah besar.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إلا» لكن أبقيناه «رحمة من ربك إن فضله كان عليك كبيرا» عظيما حيث أنزله عليك وأعطاك المقام المحمود وغير ذلك من الفضائل
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِلَّا رَحۡمَةٗ مِّن رَّبِّكَۚ إِنَّ فَضۡلَهُۥ كَانَ عَلَيۡكَ كَبِيرٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kecuali) tetapi sengaja Kami menetapkannya (karena rahmat dari Rabbmu. Sesungguhnya karunia-Nya atasmu adalah besar) agung karena Dia telah menurunkan Alquran kepadamu dan Dia memberimu kedudukan yang terpuji serta keutamaan-keutamaan lain yang Dia berikan kepadamu

ar: قُل لَّئِنِ ٱجۡتَمَعَتِ ٱلۡإِنسُ وَٱلۡجِنُّ عَلَىٰۤ أَن یَأۡتُوا۟ بِمِثۡلِ هَـٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ لَا یَأۡتُونَ بِمِثۡلِهِۦ وَلَوۡ كَانَ بَعۡضُهُمۡ لِبَعۡضࣲ ظَهِیرࣰا
ar_simple: قل لئن ٱجتمعت ٱلإنس وٱلجن على أن یأتوا بمثل هذا ٱلقرءان لا یأتون بمثله ولو كان بعضهم لبعض ظهیرا

t.sahih-int: Say, "If mankind and the jinn gathered in order to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like of it, even if they were to each other assistants
t.asad: Say: "If all mankind and all invisible beings would come together with a view to producing the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce its like even though they were to exert all their strength in aiding one another
t.yusuf-ali: Say: "If the whole of mankind and Jinns were to gather together to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like thereof, even if they backed up each other with help and support
t.pickthall: Say: Verily, though mankind and the jinn should assemble to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like thereof though they were helpers one of another
t.arberry: Say: 'If men and jinn banded together to produce the like of this Koran, they would never produce its like, not though they backed one another
t.hilali-khan: Say: "If the mankind and the jinns were together to produce the like of this Quran, they could not produce the like thereof, even if they helped one another
t.itani: Say, “If mankind and jinn came together to produce the like of this Quran, they could never produce the like of it, even if they backed up one another.”
t.mubarakpuri: Say: "If mankind and the Jinn were together to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like thereof, even if they helped one another."
t.qarib: Say, "If all people and all Jinn would band together with the intent of generating the likes of this Qur'an, they, would never be able to come out with anything like it, even if they backed each other up
t.wahiduddin: Say, "If all men and jinn gathered together to produce the like of this Quran, they could not produce one like it, however much they helped one another
t.shakir: Say: If men and jinn should combine together to bring the like of this Quran, they could not bring the like of it, though some of them were aiders of others
t.haleem: Say, ‘Even if all mankind and jinn came together to produce something like this Quran, they could not produce anything like it, however much they helped each other.’
t.maariful: Say, “If all the humans and jinns join together to produce anything like this Qur’ān, they will not (be able to) come up with anything like it, even if they assist one another
t.khattab: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “If ˹all˺ humans and jinn were to come together to produce the equivalent of this Quran, they could not produce its equal, no matter how they supported each other.”
t.ghali: Say, "Indeed in case humankind and the jinn gathered together to come up with the like of this Qur'an, they would not come up with its like, even if they were backers of one another
t.irving: SAY: "Even if men and sprites organized to produce something like this Reading, they would never bring anything like it no matter how much assistance they lent one another
t.iwq: Say, "If mankind and the jinn gathered in order to produce the like of this Qur'an, they could not produce the like of it, even if they were to each other assistants

t_id.kemenag: Katakanlah: "Sesungguhnya jika manusia dan jin berkumpul untuk membuat yang serupa Al Quran ini, niscaya mereka tidak akan dapat membuat yang serupa dengan dia, sekalipun sebagian mereka menjadi pembantu bagi sebagian yang lain".
t_id.sabiq: Katakanlah kepada mereka dan tantanglah agar mereka mendatangkan sesuatu yang serupa al-Qur'ân, dan mereka pasti tidak akan mempu mendatangkannya, "Seandainya manusia dan jin bergabung dan saling membantu untuk mendatangkan sesuatu yang serupa al-Qur'ân ini dalam komposisi dan maknanya, mereka tidak akan mampu melakukannya, meskipun mereka bekerjasama dan saling membantu satu sama lain."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah, "Sesungguhnya jika manusia dan jin berkumpul untuk membuat yang serupa dengan Alquran ini) dalam hal kefasihan dan ketinggian paramasasteranya (niscaya mereka tidak akan dapat membuat yang serupa dengan dia sekali pun sebagian mereka menjadi pembantu bagi sebagian yang lain.") saling bantu-membantu. Ayat ini diturunkan sebagai sanggahan terhadap perkataan mereka sebagaimana yang disitir oleh firman-Nya: "Kalau kami menghendaki niscaya kami dapat membacakan yang seperti ini (Alquran)." (Q.S. Al-Anfal 31).
t_id.iwq: Katakanlah: "Sesungguhnya jika manusia dan jin berkumpul untuk membuat yang serupa Al Al-Quran ini, niscaya mereka tidak akan dapat membuat yang serupa dengan dia, sekalipun sebagian mereka menjadi pembantu bagi sebagian yang lain".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (قل لئن اجتمعت الإنس والجن على أن يأتوا بمثل هذا القرآن) في الفصاحة والبلاغة. (لا يأتون بمثله ولو كان بعضهم لبعض ظهيرا) معينا نزل ردا لقولهم "" ولو نشاء لقلنا مثل هذا
tafsir.siraj-ar: قُل لَّئِنِ ٱجۡتَمَعَتِ ٱلۡإِنسُ وَٱلۡجِنُّ عَلَىٰٓ أَن يَأۡتُواْ بِمِثۡلِ هَٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ لَا يَأۡتُونَ بِمِثۡلِهِۦ وَلَوۡ كَانَ بَعۡضُهُمۡ لِبَعۡضٖ ظَهِيرٗا (ظَهِيرًا: مُعِينًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Katakanlah, "Sesungguhnya jika manusia dan jin berkumpul untuk membuat yang serupa dengan Alquran ini) dalam hal kefasihan dan ketinggian paramasasteranya (niscaya mereka tidak akan dapat membuat yang serupa dengan dia sekali pun sebagian mereka menjadi pembantu bagi sebagian yang lain.") saling bantu-membantu. Ayat ini diturunkan sebagai sanggahan terhadap perkataan mereka sebagaimana yang disitir oleh firman-Nya: "Kalau kami menghendaki niscaya kami dapat membacakan yang seperti ini (Alquran)." (Q.S. Al-Anfal)

ar: وَلَقَدۡ صَرَّفۡنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِی هَـٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ مِن كُلِّ مَثَلࣲ فَأَبَىٰۤ أَكۡثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورࣰا
ar_simple: ولقد صرفنا للناس فی هذا ٱلقرءان من كل مثل فأبى أكثر ٱلناس إلا كفورا

t.sahih-int: And We have certainly diversified for the people in this Qur'an from every [kind] of example, but most of the people refused [anything] except disbelief
t.asad: For, indeed, many facets have We given in this Qur'an to every kind of lesson [designed] for [the benefit of] mankind! However, most men are unwilling to accept anything but blasphemy
t.yusuf-ali: And We have explained to man, in this Qur'an, every kind of similitude: yet the greater part of men refuse (to receive it) except with ingratitude
t.pickthall: And verily We have displayed for mankind in this Qur'an all kind of similitudes, but most of mankind refuse aught save disbelief
t.arberry: We have indeed turned about for men in this Koran every manner of similitude; yet most men refuse all but unbelief
t.hilali-khan: And indeed We have fully explained to mankind, in this Quran, every kind of similitude, but most mankind refuse (the truth and accept nothing) but disbelief
t.itani: We have displayed for mankind in this Quran every kind of similitude, but most people insist on denying the truth.
t.mubarakpuri: And indeed We have fully explained to mankind, in this Qur'an, every kind of similitude, but most of mankind refuse (the truth and accept nothing) but disbelief.
t.qarib: We have mentioned in this Qur'an every kind of example to benefit people. However, most of the people persist in rejecting the truth
t.wahiduddin: In this Quran, We have set out all kinds of examples for people, yet most of them persist in denying the truth
t.shakir: And certainly We have explained for men in this Quran every kind of similitude, but most men do not consent to aught but denying
t.haleem: In this Quran, We have set out all kinds of examples for people, yet most of them persist in disbelieving
t.maariful: Indeed We explained every subject in this Qur’ān in various ways for the benefit of mankind. Still, most of the people refused to do anything but reject
t.khattab: And We have truly set forth every ˹kind of˺ lesson for humanity in this Quran, yet most people persist in disbelief
t.ghali: And indeed We have already propounded for mankind in this Qur'an every type of similitude; yet most of mankind have refused (all) except constant disbelief
t.irving: We have set forth every [kind of] parable for mankind in this Reading; yet most men refuse to do anything except disbelieve
t.iwq: And We have certainly diversified for the people in this Qur'an from every kind of example, but most of the people refused anything except disbelief

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah mengulang-ulang kepada manusia dalam Al Quran ini tiap-tiap macam perumpamaan, tapi kebanyakan manusia tidak menyukai kecuali mengingkari(nya).
t_id.sabiq: Dalam al-Qur'ân ini, Kami sungguh telah menempuh berbagai cara penjelasan seperti tamsil. Akan tetapi kebanyakan manusia menolaknya dan lebih memilih sikap kufur dan ingkar.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah jelaskan) telah Kami terangkan (kepada manusia dalam Alquran ini tiap-tiap macam perumpamaan) lafal min kulli matsalin menjadi sifat bagi lafal yang tidak disebutkan artinya, contoh dari setiap perumpamaan supaya mereka mengambil pelajaran darinya (tapi kebanyakan manusia tidak mau) yakni penduduk Mekah (kecuali mengingkarinya) mengingkari kebenaran yang dibawanya.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah mengulang-ulang kepada manusia dalam Al Al-Quran ini tiap-tiap macam perumpamaan, tapi kebanyakan manusia tidak menyukai kecuali mengingkari(nya).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولقد صرفنا» بينا «للناس في هذا القرآن من كل مثل» صفة لمحذوف أي مثلاً من جنس كل مثل ليتعظوا «فأبى أكثر الناس» أي أهل مكة «إلا كفورا» جحودا للحق
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَقَدۡ صَرَّفۡنَا لِلنَّاسِ فِي هَٰذَا ٱلۡقُرۡءَانِ مِن كُلِّ مَثَلٖ فَأَبَىٰٓ أَكۡثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورٗا (صَرَّفْنَا: نَوَّعْنَا وَبَيَّنَّا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah jelaskan) telah Kami terangkan (kepada manusia dalam Alquran ini tiap-tiap macam perumpamaan) lafal min kulli matsalin menjadi sifat bagi lafal yang tidak disebutkan artinya, contoh dari setiap perumpamaan supaya mereka mengambil pelajaran darinya (tapi kebanyakan manusia tidak mau) yakni penduduk Mekah (kecuali mengingkarinya) mengingkari kebenaran yang dibawanya

ar: وَقَالُوا۟ لَن نُّؤۡمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّىٰ تَفۡجُرَ لَنَا مِنَ ٱلۡأَرۡضِ یَنۢبُوعًا
ar_simple: وقالوا لن نؤمن لك حتى تفجر لنا من ٱلأرض ینبوعا

t.sahih-int: And they say, "We will not believe you until you break open for us from the ground a spring
t.asad: and so they say: "[O Muhammad,] we shall not believe thee till thou cause a spring to gush forth for us from the earth
t.yusuf-ali: They say: "We shall not believe in thee, until thou cause a spring to gush forth for us from the earth
t.pickthall: And they say: We will not put faith in thee till thou cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us
t.arberry: They say, 'We will not believe thee till thou makest a spring to gush forth from the earth for us
t.hilali-khan: And they say: "We shall not believe in you (O Muhammad SAW), until you cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us
t.itani: And they said, “We will not believe in you unless you make a spring burst from the ground for us.
t.mubarakpuri: And they say: "We shall not believe in you, until you cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us;"
t.qarib: And so they say, "We will not believe you [Prophet] until you cause a spring to gush up from the earth
t.wahiduddin: They declare, "We will never believe in you until you cause a spring to flow for us from the earth
t.shakir: And they say: We will by no means believe in you until you cause a fountain to gush forth from the earth for us
t.haleem: They say, ‘We will not believe for you [Muhammad] until you make a spring gush out of the ground for us
t.maariful: They said, “We shall never believe in you unless you cause a spring to gush forth for us from the earth
t.khattab: They challenge ˹the Prophet˺, “We will never believe in you until you cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us
t.ghali: And they have said, "We will never believe you till you make a fountain to gush forth from the earth for us
t.irving: They say: "We will never believe in you unless you cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us
t.iwq: And they say, "We will not believe you until you break open for us from the ground a spring

t_id.kemenag: Dan mereka berkata: "Kami sekali-kali tidak percaya kepadamu hingga kamu memancarkan mata air dan bumi untuk kami,
t_id.sabiq: Ketika tampak mukjizat al-Qur'ân dan mereka tidak dapat menyangkal hujjah itu, mereka pun mulai meminta ayat dan mukjizat, seperti yang dilakukan oleh orang-orang yang tidak mampu berdalih dan orang-orang yang kebingungan. Mereka kemudian berkata, "Kami tidak akan beriman kepadamu, sebelum kamu mengeluarkan mata air yang terus memancar dan tak pernah habis dari bumi Mekah untuk kami.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata) diathafkan kepada lafal abaa ("Kami sekali-kali tidak percaya kepadamu sebelum engkau memancarkan sumber air dari tanah buat kami) mata air yang berlimpah airnya.
t_id.iwq: Dan mereka berkata: "Kami sekali-kali tidak percaya kepadamu hingga kamu memancarkan mata air dan bumi untuk kami,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وقالوا» عطف على أبى «لن نؤمن لك حتى تفجر لنا من الأرض ينبوعا» عينا ينبع منها الماء
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَقَالُواْ لَن نُّؤۡمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّىٰ تَفۡجُرَ لَنَا مِنَ ٱلۡأَرۡضِ يَنۢبُوعًا (يَنْبُوعًا: عَيْنًا جَارِيَةً)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka berkata) diathafkan kepada lafal abaa ("Kami sekali-kali tidak percaya kepadamu sebelum engkau memancarkan sumber air dari tanah buat kami) mata air yang berlimpah airnya

ar: أَوۡ تَكُونَ لَكَ جَنَّةࣱ مِّن نَّخِیلࣲ وَعِنَبࣲ فَتُفَجِّرَ ٱلۡأَنۡهَـٰرَ خِلَـٰلَهَا تَفۡجِیرًا
ar_simple: أو تكون لك جنة من نخیل وعنب فتفجر ٱلأنهر خللها تفجیرا

t.sahih-int: Or [until] you have a garden of palm tress and grapes and make rivers gush forth within them in force [and abundance]
t.asad: or thou have a garden of date-palms and vines and cause rivers to gush forth in their midst in a sudden rush
t.yusuf-ali: Or (until) thou have a garden of date trees and vines, and cause rivers to gush forth in their midst, carrying abundant water
t.pickthall: Or thou have a garden of date-palms and grapes, and cause rivers to gush forth therein abundantly
t.arberry: or till thou possessest a garden of plants and vines, and thou makest rivers to gush forth abundantly all amongst it
t.hilali-khan: Or you have a garden of date-palms and grapes, and cause rivers to gush forth in their midst abundantly
t.itani: Or you have a garden of palms and vines; then cause rivers to gush pouring through them.
t.mubarakpuri: "Or you have a garden of date palms and grapes, and cause rivers to gush forth in their midst abundantly;"
t.qarib: or, unless you have a garden of dates and grapes and cause rivers to pour through it abundantly
t.wahiduddin: or you have a garden of date palms and vines, and cause streams to flow plentifully in the midst of them
t.shakir: Or you should have a garden of palms and grapes in the midst of which you should cause rivers to flow forth, gushing out
t.haleem: or until you have a garden of date palms and vines, and make rivers pour through them
t.maariful: Or you have a garden of date palms and grapes, then you bring forth rivers from their midst in abundance
t.khattab: or until you have a garden of palm trees and vineyards, and cause rivers to flow abundantly in it
t.ghali: Or (till) you have a garden of palms and vine (s); then you make rivers to erupt forth amidst it abundantly (Literally: with "all manner of" eruption)
t.irving: or have a garden full of datepalms and grapevines and make rivers gush forth plentifully through the midst of them
t.iwq: Or until you have a garden of palm tress and grapes and make rivers gush forth within them in force and abundance

t_id.kemenag: atau kamu mempunyai sebuah kebun korma dan anggur, lalu kamu alirkan sungai-sungai di celah kebun yang deras alirannya,
t_id.sabiq: Atau sebelum kamu mempunyai sebuah kebun korma dan anggur di Mekah, lalu kamu alirkan sungai- sungai dengan deras di sela-sela kebun itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Atau kamu mempunyai sebuah kebun) taman yang penuh dengan pohon-pohon (kurma dan anggur lalu kamu alirkan sungai-sungai di celah-celah kebun itu) di tengah-tengah (yang deras airnya.)
t_id.iwq: atau kamu mempunyai sebuah kebun korma dan anggur, lalu kamu alirkan sungai-sungai di celah kebun yang deras alirannya,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أو تكون لك جنة» بستان «من نخيل وعنب فتفجر الأنهار خلالها» وسطها «تفجيرا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَوۡ تَكُونَ لَكَ جَنَّةٞ مِّن نَّخِيلٖ وَعِنَبٖ فَتُفَجِّرَ ٱلۡأَنۡهَٰرَ خِلَٰلَهَا تَفۡجِيرًا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Atau kamu mempunyai sebuah kebun) taman yang penuh dengan pohon-pohon (kurma dan anggur lalu kamu alirkan sungai-sungai di celah-celah kebun itu) di tengah-tengah (yang deras airnya)

ar: أَوۡ تُسۡقِطَ ٱلسَّمَاۤءَ كَمَا زَعَمۡتَ عَلَیۡنَا كِسَفًا أَوۡ تَأۡتِیَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلۡمَلَـٰۤئِكَةِ قَبِیلًا
ar_simple: أو تسقط ٱلسماء كما زعمت علینا كسفا أو تأتی بٱلله وٱلملئكة قبیلا

t.sahih-int: Or you make the heaven fall upon us in fragments as you have claimed or you bring Allah and the angels before [us]
t.asad: or thou cause the skies to fall down upon us in smithereens, as thou hast threatened, or [till] thou bring God and the angels face to face before us
t.yusuf-ali: Or thou cause the sky to fall in pieces, as thou sayest (will happen), against us; or thou bring Allah and the angels before (us) face to face
t.pickthall: Or thou cause the heaven to fall upon us piecemeal, as thou hast pretended, or bring Allah and the angels as a warrant
t.arberry: or till thou makest heaven to fall, as thou assertest, on us in fragments, or thou bringest God and the angels as a surety
t.hilali-khan: Or you cause the heaven to fall upon us in pieces, as you have pretended, or you bring Allah and the angels before (us) face to face
t.itani: Or make the sky fall on us in pieces, as you claim, or bring God and the angels before us.
t.mubarakpuri: "Or you cause the heaven to fall upon us in pieces, as you have pretended, or you bring Allah and the angels before (us) face to face;"
t.qarib: Or, until the skies fall down upon us-crushing us, as you have threatened to do. Or [until] you bring God and the angels face to face before us
t.wahiduddin: or you cause the heavens to fall down on us in pieces, as you have claimed; or you bring God and the angels before us face to face
t.shakir: Or you should cause the heaven to come down upon us in pieces as you think, or bring Allah and the angels face to face (with us)
t.haleem: or make the sky fall on us in pieces, as you claimed will happen; or bring God and the angels before us face to face
t.maariful: Or you cause the sky to fall upon us in pieces, as you claimed, or you bring Allah and angels before us face to face
t.khattab: or cause the sky to fall upon us in pieces, as you have claimed, or bring Allah and the angels before us, face to face
t.ghali: Or you make the heaven to fall down, as you have asserted, on us in lumps, or you come up with Allah, and the Angels and their (dependent) tribes
t.irving: or you cause the sky to fall in pieces on us just as you claim, bring God and His angels to vouch for you
t.iwq: Or you make the heaven fall upon us in fragments as you have claimed or you bring Allah and the angels before us

t_id.kemenag: atau kamu jatuhkan langit berkeping-keping atas kami, sebagaimana kamu katakan atau kamu datangkan Allah dan malaikat-malaikat berhadapan muka dengan kami.
t_id.sabiq: Atau sebelum kamu runtuhkan langit menjadi berkeping-keping di atas kepala kami seperti kamu katakan bahwa Allah menjanjikan kami dengan semua itu. Atau kamu mendatangkan Allah dan malaikat- malaikat berhadapan muka dengan kami.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Atau kamu jatuhkan langit berkeping-keping atas kami sebagaimana kamu katakan) yakni hancur berkeping-keping (atau kamu datangkan Allah dan malaikat-malaikat berhadapan muka dengan kami.) secara berhadap-hadapan dan terang-terangan dengan kami sehingga kami dapat melihat mereka.
t_id.iwq: atau kamu jatuhkan langit berkeping-keping atas kami, sebagaimana kamu katakan atau kamu datangkan Allah dan malaikat-malaikat berhadapan muka dengan kami.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أو تسقط السماء كما زعمت علينا كسفا» قطعا «أو تأتي بالله والملائكة قبيلاً» مقابلة وعيانا فنراهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَوۡ تُسۡقِطَ ٱلسَّمَآءَ كَمَا زَعَمۡتَ عَلَيۡنَا كِسَفًا أَوۡ تَأۡتِيَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلۡمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ قَبِيلًا (كِسَفًا: قِطَعًا, قَبِيلًا: نُشَاهِدُهُمْ مُقَابَلَةً وَعِيَانًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Atau kamu jatuhkan langit berkeping-keping atas kami sebagaimana kamu katakan) yakni hancur berkeping-keping (atau kamu datangkan Allah dan malaikat-malaikat berhadapan muka dengan kami.) secara berhadap-hadapan dan terang-terangan dengan kami sehingga kami dapat melihat mereka

ar: أَوۡ یَكُونَ لَكَ بَیۡتࣱ مِّن زُخۡرُفٍ أَوۡ تَرۡقَىٰ فِی ٱلسَّمَاۤءِ وَلَن نُّؤۡمِنَ لِرُقِیِّكَ حَتَّىٰ تُنَزِّلَ عَلَیۡنَا كِتَـٰبࣰا نَّقۡرَؤُهُۥۗ قُلۡ سُبۡحَانَ رَبِّی هَلۡ كُنتُ إِلَّا بَشَرࣰا رَّسُولࣰا
ar_simple: أو یكون لك بیت من زخرف أو ترقى فی ٱلسماء ولن نؤمن لرقیك حتى تنزل علینا كتبا نقرؤه قل سبحان ربی هل كنت إلا بشرا رسولا

t.sahih-int: Or you have a house of gold or you ascend into the sky. And [even then], we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down to us a book we may read." Say, "Exalted is my Lord! Was I ever but a human messenger
t.asad: or thou have a house [made] of gold, or thou ascend to heaven - but nay, we would not [even] believe in thy ascension unless thou bring down to us [from heaven] a writing which we [ourselves] could read! Say thou, [O Prophet:] "Limitless in His glory is my Sustainer! Am I, then, aught but a mortal man, an apostle
t.yusuf-ali: Or thou have a house adorned with gold, or thou mount a ladder right into the skies. No, we shall not even believe in thy mounting until thou send down to us a book that we could read." Say: "Glory to my Lord! Am I aught but a man,- a messenger
t.pickthall: Or thou have a house of gold; or thou ascend up into heaven, and even then we will put no faith in thine ascension till thou bring down for us a book that we can read. Say (O Muhammad): My Lord be Glorified! Am I aught save a mortal messenger
t.arberry: or till thou possessest a house of gold ornament, or till thou goest up into heaven; and we will not believe thy going up till thou bringest down on us a book that we may read. Say: 'Glory be to my Lord. Am I aught but a mortal, a Messenger
t.hilali-khan: Or you have a house of adornable materials (like silver and pure gold, etc.), or you ascend up into the sky, and even then we will put no faith in your ascension until you bring down for us a Book that we would read." Say (O Muhammad SAW): "Glorified (and Exalted) be my Lord (Allah) above all that evil they (polytheists) associate with Him! Am I anything but a man, sent as a Messenger
t.itani: Or you possess a house of gold. Or you ascend into the sky. Even then, we will not believe in your ascension, unless you bring down for us a book that we can read.” Say, “Glory be to my Lord. Am I anything but a human messenger?”
t.mubarakpuri: "Or you have a house of Zukhruf, or you ascend up into the sky, and even then we will put no faith in your ascension until you bring down for us a Book that we would read." Say: "Glorified be my Lord! Am I anything but a man, sent as a Messenger"
t.qarib: or, to have a house of gold or you go to heaven-but we would not even believe in your ascension unless you return with a book that we can read! "Say, "May my Lord be exalted in His glory, am I other than a man appointed as a messenger
t.wahiduddin: or you have a house made of gold; or you ascend to heaven; and we will not believe in your ascension until you send down to us a Book that we can read." Tell them, "Holy is my Lord. I am but a human being sent as a messenger
t.shakir: Or you should have a house of gold, or you should ascend into heaven, and we will not believe in your ascending until you bring down to us a book which we may read. Say: Glory be to my Lord; am I aught but a mortal apostle
t.haleem: or have a house made of gold; or ascend into the sky- even then, we will not believe in your ascension until you send a real book down for us to read.’ Say, ‘Glory be to my Lord! Am I anything but a mortal, a messenger?’
t.maariful: Or you have a house made of gold. Or you ascend to the sky, and we will not believe in your ascension unless you send down to us a book we may read.” Say, “I proclaim the Purity of my Lord. I am nothing but human, a messenger.”
t.khattab: or until you have a house of gold, or you ascend into heaven—and even then we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down to us a book that we can read.” Say, “Glory be to my Lord! Am I not only a human messenger?”
t.ghali: Or you have a home of wonderful decoration, or you ascend into the heaven, and we will never believe your ascent till you keep sending down on us a book that we (can) read." Say, All Extolment be to my Lord! Have I been anything except a mortal, as Messenger
t.irving: or you have a luxurious house built, or soar up into the sky; we will never believe in your climbing up there until you bring down a book for us to read." SAY: ´Glory be to my Lord! Am I anything but a human messenger
t.iwq: Or you have a house of gold or you ascend into the sky. And even then, we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down to us a book we may read." Say, "Exalted is my Lord! Was I ever but a human messenger

t_id.kemenag: Atau kamu mempunyai sebuah rumah dari emas, atau kamu naik ke langit. Dan kami sekali-kali tidak akan mempercayai kenaikanmu itu hingga kamu turunkan atas kami sebuah kitab yang kami baca". Katakanlah: "Maha Suci Tuhanku, bukankah aku ini hanya seorang manusia yang menjadi rasul?"
t_id.sabiq: Atau sebelum kamu mempunyai sebuah rumah yang perhiasannya terbuat dari emas, atau kamu dapat naik ke atas lamgit. Kami sekali-kali tidak akan mempercayai hal itu kecuali jika kamu menurunkan kepada kami sebuah kitab suci dari Allah yang berisi kebenaran kata-katamu yang dapat kami baca." Katakanlah kepada mereka, "Tuhanku Mahasuci dari apa yang telah mereka tuduhkan bahwa di sana ada yang mengendalikan Allah atau menyamai kekuasaan-Nya. Aku ini hanyalah seorang manusia biasa, seperti layaknya para rasul yang lain, yang tidak dapat mendatangkan satu ayat pun kecuali atas izin Allah."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Atau kamu mempunyai rumah dari emas) logam mulia (atau kamu memanjat) naik (ke langit) dengan memakai tangga (Dan kami sekali-kali tidak akan mempercayai kenaikanmu) seandainya kamu dapat menaiki langit (hingga kamu turunkan atas kami) dari langit itu (sebuah kitab) yang di dalamnya tertera tulisan yang membenarkanmu (yang kami baca." Katakanlah) kepada mereka! ("Maha Suci Rabbku) ungkapan rasa takjub (bukankah) tidak lain (aku ini hanyalah manusia yang menjadi rasul.") sama halnya dengan rasul-rasul yang lain sedangkan mereka tidak dapat mendatangkan suatu mukjizat pun melainkan dengan seizin Allah.
t_id.iwq: Atau kamu mempunyai sebuah rumah dari emas, atau kamu naik ke langit. Dan kami sekali-kali tidak akan mempercayai kenaikanmu itu hingga kamu turunkan atas kami sebuah kitab yang kami baca". Katakanlah: "Maha Suci Tuhanku, bukankah aku ini hanya seorang manusia yang menjadi rasul?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أو يكون لك بيت من زخرف» ذهب «أو ترقى» تصعد «في السماء» بسُلَّم «ولن نؤمن لرقيك» لو رقيت فيها «حتى تنزل علينا» منها «كتابا» فيه تصديقك «نقرؤه قل» لهم «سبحان ربي» تعجب «هل» ما «كنت إلا بشرا رسولاً» كسائر الرسل ولم يكونوا يأتون بآية إلا بإذن الله
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَوۡ يَكُونَ لَكَ بَيۡتٞ مِّن زُخۡرُفٍ أَوۡ تَرۡقَىٰ فِي ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَلَن نُّؤۡمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ حَتَّىٰ تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيۡنَا كِتَٰبٗا نَّقۡرَؤُهُۥۗ قُلۡ سُبۡحَانَ رَبِّي هَلۡ كُنتُ إِلَّا بَشَرٗا رَّسُولٗا (زُخْرُفٍ: ذَهَبٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Atau kamu mempunyai rumah dari emas) logam mulia (atau kamu memanjat) naik (ke langit) dengan memakai tangga (Dan kami sekali-kali tidak akan mempercayai kenaikanmu) seandainya kamu dapat menaiki langit (hingga kamu turunkan atas kami) dari langit itu (sebuah kitab) yang di dalamnya tertera tulisan yang membenarkanmu (yang kami baca." Katakanlah) kepada mereka! ("Maha Suci Rabbku) ungkapan rasa takjub (bukankah) tidak lain (aku ini hanyalah manusia yang menjadi rasul.") sama halnya dengan rasul-rasul yang lain sedangkan mereka tidak dapat mendatangkan suatu mukjizat pun melainkan dengan seizin Allah

ar: وَمَا مَنَعَ ٱلنَّاسَ أَن یُؤۡمِنُوۤا۟ إِذۡ جَاۤءَهُمُ ٱلۡهُدَىٰۤ إِلَّاۤ أَن قَالُوۤا۟ أَبَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ بَشَرࣰا رَّسُولࣰا
ar_simple: وما منع ٱلناس أن یؤمنوا إذ جاءهم ٱلهدى إلا أن قالوا أبعث ٱلله بشرا رسولا

t.sahih-int: And what prevented the people from believing when guidance came to them except that they said, "Has Allah sent a human messenger
t.asad: Yet whenever [God's] guidance came to them [through a prophet,] nothing has ever kept people from believing [in him] save this their objection: "Would God have sent a [mere] mortal man as His apostle
t.yusuf-ali: What kept men back from belief when Guidance came to them, was nothing but this: they said, "Has Allah sent a man (like us) to be (His) Messenger
t.pickthall: And naught prevented mankind from believing when the guidance came unto them save that they said: Hath Allah sent a mortal as (His) messenger
t.arberry: And naught prevented men from believing when the guidance came to them, but that they said, 'Has God sent forth a mortal as Messenger
t.hilali-khan: And nothing prevented men from believing when the guidance came to them, except that they said: "Has Allah sent a man as (His) Messenger
t.itani: Nothing prevented the people from believing, when guidance has come to them, except that they said, “Did God send a human messenger?”
t.mubarakpuri: And nothing prevented men from believing when the guidance came to them, except that they said: "Has Allah sent a man as (His) Messenger"
t.qarib: Yet nothing has prevented people from believing whenever guidance was sent to them, except for their saying, " Has God sent a mere mortal man as His Messenger
t.wahiduddin: Nothing has prevented men from believing whenever guidance came to them, save their query, "Has God sent a human being as a messenger
t.shakir: And nothing prevented people from believing when the guidance came to them except that they said: What! has Allah raised up a mortal to be an apostle
t.haleem: The only thing that kept these people from believing, when guidance came to them, was that they said, ‘How could God have sent a human being as a messenger?’
t.maariful: Nothing prevented people from believing, when guidance came to them, except that they said, “Has Allah sent a man as a messenger?”
t.khattab: And nothing has prevented people from believing when guidance comes to them except their protest: “Has Allah sent a human as a messenger?”
t.ghali: And nothing (whatever) prevented mankind from believing (i.e., made them not to believe) as the guidance came to them, except that they said, "Has Allah sent forth a mortal as Messenger
t.irving: Nothing prevents men from believing whenever guidance comes to them except that they say: "Has God dispatched a mortal as a messenger
t.iwq: And what prevented the people from believing when guidance came to them except that they said, "Has Allah sent a human messenger

t_id.kemenag: Dan tidak ada sesuatu yang menghalangi manusia untuk beriman tatkala datang petunjuk kepadanya, kecuali perkataan mereka: "Adakah Allah mengutus seorang manusia menjadi rasuI?"
t_id.sabiq: Tidak ada sesuatu yang menghalangi orang-orang musyrik Mekah untuk mengakui kebenaran saat didatangkan wahyu yang diikuti dengan mukjizat, kecuali ungkapan bodoh mereka yang mengatakan bahwa Allah tidak akan mengutus para rasul-Nya dari jenis manusia, tetapi dari jenis malaikat.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidak ada sesuatu yang menghalang-halangi manusia untuk beriman tatkala datang petunjuk kepadanya kecuali perkataan mereka) perkataan mereka dengan nada ingkar ("Adakah Allah mengutus seorang manusia menjadi rasul?") dan Dia tidak mengutus seorang malaikat?
t_id.iwq: Dan tidak ada sesuatu yang menghalangi manusia untuk beriman tatkala datang petunjuk kepadanya, kecuali perkataan mereka: "Adakah Allah mengutus seorang manusia menjadi rasuI?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وما منع الناس أن يؤمنوا إذ جاءهم الهدى إلا أن قالوا» أي قولهم منكرين «أبعث الله بشرا رسولاً» ولم يبعث ملكا
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمَا مَنَعَ ٱلنَّاسَ أَن يُؤۡمِنُوٓاْ إِذۡ جَآءَهُمُ ٱلۡهُدَىٰٓ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوٓاْ أَبَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ بَشَرٗا رَّسُولٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidak ada sesuatu yang menghalang-halangi manusia untuk beriman tatkala datang petunjuk kepadanya kecuali perkataan mereka) perkataan mereka dengan nada ingkar ("Adakah Allah mengutus seorang manusia menjadi rasul?") dan Dia tidak mengutus seorang malaikat

ar: قُل لَّوۡ كَانَ فِی ٱلۡأَرۡضِ مَلَـٰۤئِكَةࣱ یَمۡشُونَ مُطۡمَئِنِّینَ لَنَزَّلۡنَا عَلَیۡهِم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَاۤءِ مَلَكࣰا رَّسُولࣰا
ar_simple: قل لو كان فی ٱلأرض ملئكة یمشون مطمئنین لنزلنا علیهم من ٱلسماء ملكا رسولا

t.sahih-int: Say, "If there were upon the earth angels walking securely, We would have sent down to them from the heaven an angel [as a] messenger
t.asad: Say: "If angels were walking about on earth as their natural abode, We would indeed have sent down unto them an angel out of heaven as Our apostle
t.yusuf-ali: Say, "If there were settled, on earth, angels walking about in peace and quiet, We should certainly have sent them down from the heavens an angel for a messenger
t.pickthall: Say: If there were in the earth angels walking secure, We had sent down for them from heaven an angel as messenger
t.arberry: Say: 'Had there been in the earth angels walking. at peace, We would have sent down upon them out of heaven an angel as Messenger
t.hilali-khan: Say: "If there were on the earth, angels walking about in peace and security, We should certainly have sent down for them from the heaven an angel as a Messenger
t.itani: Say, “If there were angels on earth, walking around in peace, We would have sent down to them from heaven an angel messenger.”
t.mubarakpuri: Say: "If there were on the earth, angels walking about in peace and security, We should certainly have sent down for them from the heaven an angel as a Messenger."
t.qarib: Say, "If angels walked the earth feeling at home, We would have then sent down an angel from heaven as Our Messenger
t.wahiduddin: Say, "If there had been angels walking around on earth, We would have sent an angel down from Heaven as a messenger for them
t.shakir: Say: Had there been in the earth angels walking about as settlers, We would certainly have sent down to them from the heaven an angel as an apostle
t.haleem: Say, ‘If there were angels walking about on earth, feeling at home, We would have sent them an angel from Heaven as a messenger.’
t.maariful: Say, “Had there been angels (living) on the earth, walking about in peace, We would have certainly sent down an angel from the heavens as a messenger.”
t.khattab: Say, ˹O Prophet,˺ “Had there been angels walking the earth, well settled, We would have surely sent down for them an angel from heaven as a messenger.”
t.ghali: Say, "If there were in the earth Angels walking composed, indeed We would have been sending down upon them from the heaven an Angel as a Messenger
t.irving: SAY: "If there had been angels calmly walking around on earth, we would have sent an angel down from Heaven as a messenger for them
t.iwq: Say, "If there were upon the earth angels walking securely, We would have sent down to them from the heaven an angel as a messenger
